series_metadata.series_description: Reports, correspondence, or other information required to be sent to the Office of the Governor (OOG) by statute or rule that require no action on the part of OOG.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Follow statute or rule if a
longer retention is required.

series_metadata.series_description: Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and other correspondence and supporting documentation related to the Sunset review process for state agencies.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission’s draft
report and documentation of the
agency’s implementation of the
commission’s final
recommendations. The final version
of the agency self-evaluation report
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_metadata.series_description: Complaints and other unsolicited feedback received by an agency from the public concerning the agency and records pertaining to the resolution of complaints.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of
agency item number 98. Does not
include employee complaints; SEE
item number 151.

series_metadata.series_description: Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media, including electronic communication, pertaining to or arising from the routine operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of an agency. correspondence, in any format.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include general
correspondence sent or received by
the Governor; See agency item
number 80.

series_metadata.series_title: Calendars, Appointment and Itinerary Records - Executive
series_metadata.series_description: Calendars, appointment books, and scheduling/itinerary records, maintained by staff during business hours, that document the activities of elected officials, executive administration, board or commission members, and division directors. Includes, but is not limited to, the Governor's daily schedule.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
agency heads, and board or
commission members require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_metadata.series_title: Calendars, Appointment and Itinerary Records - General
series_metadata.series_description: Calendars, appointment books, and scheduling/itinerary records, maintained by staff during business hours, that document the activities of agency employees. Also includes common calendars used for coordinating work schedules/assignments or reserving conference rooms.

retention_rules.disposition: Calendar Year End plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number 84a for
calendars of directors, board
members, and executive
administration.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_metadata.series_description: News, press releases, collateral, or any public relations files maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records. records.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 102 (Transitory
Information) for images that are no
longer deemed necessary or are
unusable (as extraneous, unusable,
almost-duplicates, etc.).

series_metadata.series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of the agency and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
agency need to be transferred to
the State Archives. For
division/department level
organization charts, see item
number 89b.

series_metadata.series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of a division and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded

series_metadata.series_description: Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services, or projects of an agency that are not included in or directly related to other records series in this schedule.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Surveys soliciting feedback from customers, clients, or stakeholders of an agency, and the statistical data maintained rating an agency's performance.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 112 (Reports and
Studies (Non-Fiscal)) for summary
reports compiled from customer
surveys.

series_metadata.series_description: Instructional materials and other records developed by an agency associated with training the entities or non-personnel individuals it regulates or serves, including but not limited to training manuals, course registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets, course outlines, and similar training aids used in external training programs.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include Crime
Stoppers training; SEE item number
5d. SEE item number 185 for
internal training materials.
SEE ALSO item number 63 (Event
Administration Records).

series_metadata.series_description: Operational strategic plans prepared in accordance with Government Code § 481.172(a)(8) (Tourism) or § 2308.104 (TWIC). Also includes the long-range strategic plan authorized by Government Code § 2056.009, as applicable.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 6 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Self-evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans with Disabilities Act.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 101b for ADA
Accommodation Requests.

series_metadata.series_title: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Accommodation Requests
series_metadata.series_description: Employee/applicant requests for reasonable accommodation under the ADA.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, taken at internal agency staff meetings.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Intended for meeting
summaries, substantive notes or
reports and supporting
documentation from internal
meetings. Individual reminder notes
or reference notes made by
individuals may possibly be
transitory or reference material.
SEE item number 102.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports and Studies (Non- Fiscal), Raw Data
series_metadata.series_description: Information or data collected and compiled for the purpose of producing non- fiscal reports.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 109 (Agency
Performance Measures
Documentation).

series_metadata.series_description: Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required by an agency's enabling statutes, including annual narrative reports if they are required by statute. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating final report.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 6 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.
Includes reports required by
Education Code § 62.168;
Government Code §§ 436.103,
481.005(d), 481.0066(d-2),
481.00681(h), 481.079, 481.412,
485A.053, 489.107, 490G.007,
772.006(a)(9), 772.0062(d)(6),
772.007(d), 2303.052,
2308.101(a)(14), 2308.101(b),
2308.1015(b), 2308.1016(b),
2310.052; and Human Resources
Code § 115.009(3).

series_metadata.series_description: Quarterly and annual reports on agency performance measures submitted to the executive and legislative offices.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 6 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 109 (Agency
Performance Measures
Documentation) for supporting
documentation.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports compiled by agency personnel on a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to workload monitoring, task completion times, number of public contacts, and similar activities.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: See item number 109
(Agency Performance Measures
Documentation) if the reports are
used to document performance
measures.

series_metadata.series_description: Information in any format that is publicly distributed by the agency, statutorily required to be distributed by the agency, or produced by the authority of or at the expense of the agency. The term does not include information distributed solely to internal staff, staff of other government agencies, or contractors/grantees of the agency. Some examples of non-archival publications include brochures, announcements, artwork, and internal newsletters.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Publications
Depository rules are
outlined in 13 TAC §§
3.1-.16

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
§§3.1-3.8).

series_metadata.series_description: Lists of mailing addresses, telephone/fax numbers, or e-mail addresses maintained by an agency on its employees or on entities or persons it serves.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Records of this nature,
particularly those in databases or
other information systems, may be
overwritten; disposal does not need
to be documented through records
disposition logs.

series_metadata.series_description: Records and reports of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Any record created by the agency to track any type of incoming and outgoing correspondence or packages by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) or by private couriers.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include Indigov
database; see item numbers 130
(Databases and Information
Systems) and 80 (Executive
Correspondence).
SEE ALSO item number 231
(Directories).

series_metadata.series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Criminal history record information on job applicants or agency employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_metadata.series_description: Projects conducted by agency staff whose records do not fit under another record series grouping. Includes notes, reference materials, work papers, meeting minutes, correspondence related to the project, and any other supporting documentation needed throughout the duration of the project.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records or forms designed and used solely for data input and control.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Requisitions for In-Agency or Inter-Agency Copy/Printing Service
series_metadata.series_description: Includes word processing and data processing.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Formal agreements between the OOG and other entities to share resources for mutual benefit. May include Memoranda of Understanding (MOU), Memoranda of Agreement (MOA), licensing agreements, and location agreements.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
contracts listed under item number
229 or grant agreements (SEE
Section 4.6.1). Also does not
include ceremonial MOUs signed by
the Governor (SEE item number 83,
Executive Orders).

series_metadata.series_description: Content (messages, posts, photographs, videos, etc.) created or received using a social media application.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Contact the RMO if an
account is to be deactivated or
deleted. Social media content must
remain accessible until retention is
met.

series_metadata.series_title: Audiovisual Recordings or Certified Agendas - Closed Meetings
series_metadata.series_description: Certified agendas or audiovisual recordings of closed meetings (executive sessions) of boards, commissions, committees, councils, or task forces.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Government Code §
551.104(a) for state
boards, commissions,
and other entities
considered
governmental bodies
under the Texas
Open Meetings Act.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Meeting Notes - State Boards, Commissions, and Councils
series_metadata.series_description: Notes or transcriptions taken during meetings of boards, commissions, committees, councils, or task forces from which written minutes are prepared. May include notes taken at advisory committee meetings.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 3 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 108 for staff
meeting minutes or notes.

series_metadata.series_description: Meeting agendas, minutes, and board packets/supporting documentation of committees and boards that are strictly advisory and do not control or supervise public business or policy. Include, but are not limited to, records of the Aerospace and Aviation Advisory Committee, Crime Stoppers Council, Governor’s Commission for Women, Governor’s University Research Initiative (GURI) Advisory Board, Office of Small Business Assistance Advisory Task Force, Specialty Courts Advisory Council, Star of Texas Awards Advisory Committees, and Texas Military Value Task Force.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 103 for
agendas/minutes of committees
required to conduct meetings in
accordance with Government Code,
Chapter 551; item number 105 for
audiovisual recordings; and item
number 325 for records of
nonprofit boards.
NOTE: In the event of the
abolishment of the Texas Military
Value Task Force, task force records
should be transferred to the Texas
Military Preparedness Commission
in accordance with § 436.1051,
Government Code.

series_metadata.series_description: Witness registration forms, sign-in sheets, or similar documents filled out by members of the public or other state agencies requesting to speak at an open meeting or budget hearing.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: If all information from the
sign-in sheet is documented in
written minutes of the meeting, the
sign-in sheet need only be retained
for the retention period listed in
item number 106 (Meeting Notes).

series_metadata.series_description: Bylaws, articles of incorporation, tax- exempt status recognition documents, board meeting agendas and minutes, annual financial statements, and similar records documenting the history, structure, and governance of nonprofit boards affiliated with the Office of the Governor. Includes records of the Beacon State Fund, State Agency Council, Texas Disaster Relief Fund, Texas Governor’s Mansion Administration (TGMA), Inc., and Texas Governor's Mansion Greenhouse Project.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include the
records of state boards,
commissions, committees, councils,
task forces, or advisory committees.
SEE items 103, 104, 105, 106, 107,
299, and 321.

series_metadata.series_description: Applications for gubernatorial appointments to state agency governing boards, councils, task forces, and commissions. Includes records such as application forms, resumes, questionnaires, letters of recommendation, and disclosure forms. Includes files of both appointed and non- appointed individuals, but see Archives Note.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Files for non-
appointed individuals are not
archival.

series_metadata.series_description: This series documents requests from individuals or organizations for the Governor or First Lady to attend an event or meeting. May include, but is not limited to: invitations (with their enclosures or attachments, as applicable), correspondence, and a copy of the Governor's daily schedule. Includes both accepted and declined invitations, but see Archives Note.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Invitations received by office
staff may be classified under item
number 81 (General
Correspondence) or 102 (Transitory
Information). ARCHIVES NOTE:
Declined invitations are not
archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Any document that initiates, rescinds, or amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the programs, services, or projects of an agency.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE ALSO item number 333
(Proclamation Files).

series_metadata.series_description: Text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered by the Governor, executive staff, board or commission members, or division directors.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_metadata.series_description: Text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_metadata.series_description: Subpoenas for production of evidence produced for litigation in which the state agency is not a party. Includes legal documents requiring recipient to appear in court to testify, or to produce records to be used in litigation.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed

tracking_data.comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation
in which the state agency is a party,
see item number 98 (Litigation
Files).

series_metadata.series_description: May include copies of proposed legislation and related correspondence or analyses.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Log listing all gifts sent directly to the Office of the Governor or not associated with the GGRD (Gift, Grant, Reimbursement, or Donation) form approval process. Gifts are managed in accordance with Government Code § 401.101-102.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include GGRD
forms or any logs related to the
GGRD process. SEE item number
180a.

series_metadata.series_description: Weekly reports, prepared by staff of the Office of State-Federal Relations (OSFR), providing legislative updates and information on division activities.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Request/planning forms and related records documenting state agency functions held at the Governor’s Mansion.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Copies of financial
records retained for convenience in
the Mansion's event file (e.g.,
invoices, receipts, etc.) must be
discarded prior to transfer to the
State Archives. Financial Services is
the custodian of financial records
and will retain such records
according to the applicable records
series.

series_metadata.series_description: Files relating to the issuance of proclamations, which include a copy of the proclamation and any correspondence relating to the proclamation.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Biennial appropriations budget prepared in accordance with Government Code § 401.0445 and 401.046.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 6 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the transition from an incumbent Governor to the Governor- elect. Includes briefing materials summarizing the history, structure, operations, and information systems of each division.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records pertaining to the extradition of prisoners to or from another state within the United States. Includes applications to Governor for issuance of requisition, Governor's Requisition forms, and signature authorization memos; may also include waivers of extradition.

retention_rules.disposition: Calendar Year End plus 10 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Filed by date of receipt or
file creation, as applicable.

series_metadata.series_description: Records pertaining to the transfer of prisoners to foreign countries under the provisions of Article IV, Section 11 of the Constitution of the State of Texas. Includes applications, letters granting/denying transfer, copies of judgments, and copies of constituent communications.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 10 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Files typically include Execution Memos prepared by General Counsel; copies of court documents (orders, memoranda, addenda, etc.); correspondence with the Texas Department of Criminal Justice and Office of the Attorney General; copies of constituent communications in support of or opposition to the execution; and inmate last statements.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Files pertaining to applications for clemency for prisoners or convicted felons out on parole seeking a pardon, asking to have a sentence commuted, or asking for an emergency medical reprieve. Includes individual offenders' files and correspondence.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Misdemeanor
case files are not archival and may
be destroyed when they meet
retention.

series_metadata.series_description: Trademark records which pertain to trademarks registered on behalf of the agency. May include but not limited to a copy of the trademark itself as submitted to the United States Patent and Trademark Office and the corresponding trademark registration application, filings, notices, and supplementary documents.

retention_rules.disposition: Permanent plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Third-party briefs, which
document the agency's actions on
Public Information Act requests
received by other state agencies,
may be classified under item
number 81 (General
Correspondence). Withdrawn
requests may also be classified
under item number 81. Responsive
documents received from 3rd party
agencies are not OOG records and
should be discarded when no longer
administratively valuable.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years

series_metadata.series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records created by or on behalf of an agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit. Includes settlement packets.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Settlement packets
are maintained
pursuant to Civil
Practices and
Remedies Code §
101.105.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the State
Archives for archival preservation.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records used to register persons appearing before state agencies as required by Chapter 2004, Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 263 (Visitor
Control Registers) for visitor sign-in
sheets.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports made to the Office of Attorney General (OAG) on an agency's Public Information Act activities, per Government Code, 552.010.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Forms granting or obtaining consent for use of photographs or video footage.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Requests for the Governor's granting of suspensions of certain statutes and rules after a disaster. Includes correspondence, copies of proclamations, and the suspension list prepared in accordance with Government Code § 418.0155.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Structured sets of related data organized for rapid search and retrieval.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: All metadata required to
understand the structure of
database records must be
maintained until the retention
period of the data has been met.

series_metadata.series_description: Agency-developed automated software applications, operating system files, and associated processing files, including job control language, programs, applications, scripts, source code, etc. Also includes Access design objects (forms and reports), .Net code, and SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) reports.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.
SEE item number 133 (Technical
Documentation) for user manuals
and similar records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, and equipment control systems.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Hardware
documentation needed for access
to electronic records must be
retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing of electronic records and their timely, authorized disposition. Includes documentation describing how a system operates and which is necessary for using the system such as: * user guides, system or sub-system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, and system flowcharts; * program descriptions and documentation such as program flowcharts, program maintenance logs, change notices, and other records that document modifications to computer programs; and * data documentation necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate and interpret data in an automated system such as a data element dictionary, file layout, code book or table, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Technical documentation
needed to access or understand
electronic records (e.g., user
manuals or codebooks) must be
retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Relatively long-lived computer files containing organized and consistent sets of complete and accurate electronic records and associated processing files. Examples include, but are not limited to, data tables of relational databases used by applications or computer programs.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed

series_metadata.series_description: Project management records, design documentation, feasibility studies, justifications, user requirements, etc.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Forms created for security purposes to control or monitor individual access to a computer/network system and its data. Internal forms include, but are not limited to, Security Control (SC) forms, Network Security Rights (NSR) forms, and Social Technology Access Forms. External forms include, but are not limited to, user agreement forms submitted to the Comptroller of Public Accounts.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Tickets or work orders for computer/technical support submitted to Information Services Division (ISD) and/or Support Services Division staff.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties, and License Agreements
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the registration and licensing of a software application to activate the software for legal use by the end users of a state agency, along with warranties providing that the software will perform in accordance with functional specifications.

retention_rules.disposition: Life of Asset plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Biennial information security plan for protecting the security of the agency’s information.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded

series_metadata.series_description: Master versions of all forms used internally and externally by the agency, including subsequent revisions. May also include associated design or design modification requests.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: A records retention schedule (i.e., form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite or offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical custody.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Worksheets or working papers used to capture records inventory information, including location tracking and to document existence and requirements of a records series.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include record
survey worksheets used for
retention schedule development;
SEE item number 289.

series_metadata.series_description: Lists, indexes, finding aids, registers, and similar documents, in any format, used to locate and retrieve records.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_metadata.series_description: File plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in an agency.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113) or other entities to establish disaster recovery services, authorize agency staff to access the media, etc.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Includes record survey forms, internal and external correspondence, research files, notes, database content, and all other materials supporting revisions to the records retention schedule. Also includes schedule amendments (SLR 122), but see caution note.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Records Management
Officer must incorporate
amendments to the records
retention schedule (SLR 105) upon
receiving notification of TSLAC
approval.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes Records Management Coordinator (RMC) Designation forms and agency copy of the Records Management Officer (RMO) Designation form (SLR 104).

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the review of employee complaints regarding personnel policies, working conditions, etc.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the
U.S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number 98 (Litigation Files).

series_metadata.series_description: Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in an agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Resumes or applications received by a division that are unrelated to a specific job posting. Retention period applies if the division replies to the sender indicating that the resume will be kept on file should future job openings occur.

retention_rules.disposition: As Long as Administratively Valuable plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 290 (Job Files) for
resumes that are used in any way in
the employment selection process.
Human Resources does not accept
or retain unsolicited resumes.

series_metadata.series_description: Personnel records maintained on each intern. May include, but is not limited to, applications, eligibility verification forms, evaluations, timesheets, training and educational achievement records, and related information.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Internship applications, resumes, outside employment forms, background authorization releases, and selection records for applicants who are not selected.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Journals, notes, correspondence, kudos, or other documentation maintained on an individual employee by a supervisor. May also include telework requests, agreements, and associated documentation. Retention period applies only to records not transferred to Human Resources for inclusion in the Employee File.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Does not include
performance appraisals; SEE item
number 291 (Employee Files).

series_metadata.series_description: Includes W-2, 1095-B, 1099, FICA, and other tax records.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees.

retention_rules.disposition: Calendar Year End plus 4 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include, but is not limited to, payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
Human Resources is the custodian
of payroll vouchers.

series_metadata.series_description: Agency staffing reports (any reports compiled by the agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by program or name; staff hired during a month; detailed listings of employees within the OOG organizational structure, position vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and seasonality of employment, etc.), Human Resources Information System (HRIS) reports (EEO reports and supporting documentation, labor statistics reports, management to staff ratio reports, new hire reports), and Texas Workforce Commission (TWC) reports. May also include supporting documentation used to create reports.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
29 CFR § 1602.32 for
EEO reports and
supporting
documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
Financial Services is the custodian
of TWC reports.

series_metadata.series_description: Diversity and recruitment plans and related workforce analyses.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Employee benefit plans such as pension; life, health, and disability insurance; deferred compensation; etc., including amendments. Series comprises copies of records held by Employees Retirement System of Texas (ERS).

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Minimum information needed to verify employment; includes name, social security number, exact dates of employment, last known address, and most recent public access election information.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 75 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments: VITAL RECORD

series_metadata.series_title: Requests/Authorizations for Training or Other Reimbursable Activity
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for participation in educational programs, workshops, or college classes; or for other work-related activities for which the expenses of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Gift, Grant, Reimbursement, or Donation (GGRD) Forms
series_metadata.series_description: Requests for approval to receive a gift, grant, reimbursement, or donation in accordance with Government Code § 401.101. Also includes any logs or other records (e.g., spreadsheets) used to track the receipt and approval of GGRD forms.
series_metadata.legal_citation: Government Code § 401.101. Also includes any logs or other records (e.g., spreadsheets) used to track the receipt and approval of GGRD forms.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include the Gift
Registry Log, which lists gifts and in-
kind donations sent directly to the
office and not associated with the
GGRD form approval process; SEE
item number 295.

series_metadata.series_description: Aptitude or skills tests required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different master test (different in
terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should
be retained for the period
indicated.

series_metadata.series_title: Training Materials and Administration Records - Internal
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the planning, development, implementation, administration, and evaluation of in-house training programs, including but not limited to training manuals, course registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets, syllabuses, course outlines, and similar training aids used in in-house training programs.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
For training presented to external
entities, SEE item number 97
(Training Materials – External) and
63 (Event Administration Records).

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting individual employee’s hours worked, including work schedules and documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item 292 (Intern Files) for intern
timesheets.

series_metadata.series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Requests and Authorizations
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of authorized leave, and supporting documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting number of hours transferred in and out of comprehensive sick leave pool.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Forms used to establish vendors and grantees in the Texas Identification Number System (TINS) and internal financial systems. Includes Direct Deposit Authorizations (Comptroller Form 74-176), Texas Identification Number (TIN) applications (Comptroller Form AP-152), Payee Change Request forms (Comptroller Form 74-157), and Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification (IRS Form W-9).

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: The retention period starts
from the end of the fiscal year in
which the account is set up.
SEE item number 294
(Benefits/Payroll Files) for
employee direct deposit
authorizations.

series_metadata.series_description: Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing registers, account cards, deposit warrants, cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts books, cash transfers, daily cash reports, reconciliations, general journal vouchers, special vouchers, and similar records that serve to document money owed to or received by a state agency and its collection or receipt.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Deposit vouchers (D documents), deposit slips, copies of checks, bank teller receipts, and supporting documentation. May include copies of RR documents.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE ALSO item number 210
(Banking Records) for other banking
records such as bank statements
and check registers.

series_metadata.series_description: Encumbrance/liquidation batches and supporting documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Purchase requisitions, invoices or statements, purchase vouchers, interagency transfer vouchers, packing slips, all grant disbursements except for Texas Enterprise Fund (SEE item number 60a), and related records. Includes document types 2, 9, and T.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 229 (Contract
Administration Files) for purchase
orders.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes travel requisitions, travel vouchers, and often supporting documentation such as flight itineraries, hotel receipts, and cost calculations. Also includes travel documents for which no reimbursement was received, such as No Expenses to Claim memos or Travel Cancelled memos.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Includes budget transfers/revisions (A documents and B documents), payment cancellation vouchers (C documents), and general journal vouchers (J documents and K documents).

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Warrant registers/USAS payable reports.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Revenue, expenditure, cash, etc.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Contracts, leases, and agreements include general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction except for buildings. Documents include specifications, affidavits of publication of calls for bids, performance bonds, contracts, purchase orders, inspection reports, and correspondence. May also include other applicable documentation in the master contract file per Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts Contract Management Guide. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
VITAL RECORD. SEE ALSO item
numbers 256a, b, and c (Bid
Documentation).

series_metadata.series_description: Contracts, leases, and agreements include general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction except for buildings. Documents include specifications, affidavits of publication of calls for bids, performance bonds, contracts, purchase orders, inspection reports, and correspondence. May also include other applicable documentation in the master contract file per Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts Contract Management Guide. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years

tracking_data.comments:
VITAL RECORD. SEE ALSO item
numbers 256a, b, and c (Bid
Documentation).

series_metadata.series_description: Includes successful and unsuccessful bids, bid requisitions or authorizations, requests for proposal (RFP), invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, and bid tabulation/evaluation documentation. Applies to bids associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 256c for bids that
are withdrawn or not included in
the bid evaluation process.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes successful and unsuccessful bids, bid requisitions or authorizations, requests for proposal (RFP), invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, and bid tabulation/evaluation documentation. Applies to bids associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 256c for bids that
are withdrawn or not included in
the bid evaluation process.

series_metadata.series_description: Unsuccessful bids that are withdrawn or not included in the bid evaluation process (missed deadline, incomplete submission, etc.).

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Requests for information or quotes preliminary to the procurement of goods or services by direct purchase or bid.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: If the request for
information leads to a request for
proposal (RFP) or bid, the RFI
documentation must be retained in
accordance with item number 256a,
256b, or 256c, as applicable.

series_metadata.series_description: List of agency contracts, leases, and agreements including general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction contracts.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriation requests by the agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 6 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Copies are sent to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program at the time of
submission to the Legislative
Budget Board (LBB). Working files
and related documentation used in
creating the final plan are not
subject to archival review and may
be disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_metadata.series_title: Fiscal Management Reports and Working Papers
series_metadata.series_description: Includes internal operating budget, budget reports and analyses, cash balance reports, financial statements, PFIA reports, Bond Review Board reports, fiscal notes, or other fiscal reports or internal budget requests. Also includes working papers for such reports.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include the
Agency Operating Budget required
by the General Appropriations Act;
SEE item number 216.

series_metadata.series_description: Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of accounts.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
For internal operating budgets,
including budget working papers
created at the division/program
level, SEE item number 213
(Internal Fiscal Management
Reports).
ARCHIVES NOTE: Archival
requirement is met by sending
copies to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
TSLAC.

series_metadata.series_description: Daily USAS reports generated or received by the agency. Includes, but is not limited to, DAFR (Detailed Activity Financial Report) 2021, 3331, 3601, and 8200. Monthly USAS reports generated by the agency. Includes, but is not limited to, DAFR 5651, 5801, 5851, 7400, 7470, 7500, and 8660.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: See agency item number 207
(Expenditure Journals or Registers)
for DAFR 3691, Direct Deposit
Payees by Agency.

series_metadata.series_description: Yearly report complied from the information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Applications, grant authorization records, review and monitoring documentation, disbursement documentation, and other information related to Texas Enterprise Fund awards made by the agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the creation of programs and funding for emerging technology industries. Includes application, review and compliance documents, and RCIC (Regional Center for Innovation & Commercialization) contracts.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 5 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203
(Purchase/Payment Vouchers).
Program abolished as of September
1, 2015. Senate Bill 632, 84(R).

series_metadata.series_description: Applications, grant authorization records, grant/project financial or performance reports, review and monitoring documentation, and other information related to federally-funded grants or awards made or received by the agency. Includes criminal justice, homeland security, and Wagner-Peyser grants.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements
may vary depending on the specific
funding agency's requirements.
CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203.

series_metadata.series_description: Applications, grant authorization records, grant/project financial or performance reports, review and monitoring documentation, and other information related to state-funded grants or awards made or received by the agency. Includes state-funded criminal justice grants, homeland security grants, Texas Moving Image Industry Incentive Program (TMIIIP), Defense Economic Adjustment Assistance Grant (DEAAG), Governor's University Research Initiative (GURI), Spaceport Trust Fund, Small Business Service Provider Grants, Texas Music Office grants, and any other state grant except where noted below. CAUTION: Does not include Texas Enterprise Fund (SEE item number 60a), Texas Disaster Fund (SEE item number 218c), or Emerging Technology Fund (see item number 62a).

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203.

series_metadata.series_title: Texas Disaster Fund Grant Records - Awarded/Funded
series_metadata.series_description: Request letters, grant authorization records, review and monitoring documentation, and other information related to Texas Disaster Fund awards made by the agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
disbursement documentation; SEE
item number 203.

series_metadata.series_description: Unfunded applications for grant, award, or incentive programs (federal, state, or other). Includes evaluation documentation such as scoresheets.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Plan prepared by the Office of the Governor and distributed to each state agency as required by Government Code § 2106.002. Series includes plans submitted to and approved by the federal government, as well as supporting documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: The retention period starts
from the end of the fiscal year
covered by the plan.

series_metadata.series_description: Indirect Cost Plan and supporting documentation created or maintained in the development of the plan.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: The retention period starts
from the end of the fiscal year
covered by the plan.

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency- owned equipment and property.

retention_rules.disposition: Life of Asset plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting financial obligations of a state agency that are not payable within one year of the date of the balance sheet, including debentures, loans, deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension obligations.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Constitution
art. III, §§ 49-n, 52-a;
art. XVI, § 71. 26 CFR
§ 1.148-5(d)(6)(iii)(E)

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
purchase agreements, letters of
credit, and trust indentures; SEE
item number 229 (Contract
Administration Files). Does not
include loans made by the agency
using bond proceeds; SEE item
number 319. Does not include
Industrial Revenue Bonds; SEE item
number 34a.

series_metadata.series_description: Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of an agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Bank statements, cancelled checks, check registers, debit and credit notices, reconciliations, and other banking related records.

retention_rules.disposition: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

tracking_data.comments:
SEE ALSO item number 200
(Deposits).

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting incoming or outgoing deliveries, including through private courier services.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Bills of lading for items
transferred to the warehouse
should be classified under item
number 246 (Inventory Transfer
Forms).

series_metadata.series_description: Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1, 2015.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile, and other policies for government property and personnel including supporting documentation relevant to the implementation, modification, renewal, or replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 4 years

series_metadata.series_title: Building Plans and Specifications - State Owned
series_metadata.series_description: Includes architectural and engineering drawings, profiles, and blueprints of planning, design, construction, conversion, or modernization of state-owned facilities, structures, infrastructure, and systems.

retention_rules.disposition: Life of Asset plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE ALSO item number 237a
(Building Construction Project
Files).

series_metadata.series_title: Inspection, Repair, and Maintenance Records – Equipment
series_metadata.series_description: Equipment history file, which may include logbooks and requests for installation, moves, service, repair, etc.

retention_rules.disposition: Life of Asset plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
equipment service agreements; SEE
item number 229 (Contract
Administration Files).

retention_rules.disposition: Life of Asset plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Manuals must be retained by
the division that owns the
equipment.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Warranties must be retained
by the division that owns the
equipment.

series_metadata.series_title: Quotes for Minor Construction/Repairs (Supply and Repair Cost Estimates)
series_metadata.series_description: Quotes for electrical work, minor construction projects, etc. Includes supporting documentation, as applicable.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Agency copy of forms completed by mechanical service personnel (of TFC, DPS, DIR, etc.) for installation or repair. Also includes HelpDesk tickets or work orders submitted by OOG staff.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months
After Closed plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Vehicle titles, registration information, and owner's manuals for state vehicles.

retention_rules.disposition: Life of Asset plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
Official record maintained by
Financial Services.

series_metadata.series_description: Spreadsheets or similar logs used to assign parking spaces to employees.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Occupational Accident Reports and Associated Documentation
series_metadata.series_description: Accident or occupational disease reports (by supervisors and employees) and other associated reports required to be submitted to the State Office of Risk Management (SORM) or its predecessors or maintained internally on accident frequency.

retention_rules.disposition: Calendar Year End plus 5 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Agency copy of plans for evacuation of agency facilities in cases of emergency.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
Texas Facilities Commission (TFC)
maintains the official copies of
emergency exit and other
evacuation plans.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs, sign-in sheets, or similar records documenting visitors to limited access or restricted areas of agency facilities.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
SEE item number 99 (Registration
Logs) for a record of visitors
required to register under Chapter
2004, Government Code.

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities, equipment, or automated systems.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Incomplete CP-6 forms are
transitory and may be destroyed
after submission to the Texas
Department of Public Safety (DPS).
SEE ALSO item number 306 (IT
Security Access Forms).

series_metadata.series_description: Plans to mitigate the damage of potential events that could endanger the agency’s ability to function. Includes disaster preparedness, response, and recovery plans.

retention_rules.disposition: Until Superseded plus 0 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Audits and reviews performed by or on behalf of an agency, including the working papers that support the audit. Also includes audits performed on the agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
The State Auditor's Office (SAO)
retains copies of its audits
performed on Texas state agencies.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes working papers and agency risk assessment used to develop the plan.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Internal
Auditing Act
requirement
(Government Code §
2102.013)

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number 77
(Audits) for final audit reports.
Risk assessments prepared by the
Office of Compliance and
Monitoring should be classified
under their respective program
areas (i.e., use item 218a for federal
grants, 218b for state grants, etc.).

series_metadata.series_description: Documents collected or generated as part of the process of reviewing other state agency internal audit programs. May include but is not limited to self- assessments, worksheets, surveys or questionnaires, evaluations, and other documents as described in the State Agency Internal Audit Forum (SAIAF) Peer Review Manual.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Internal
Auditing Act
requirement
(Government Code §
2102.007(5))

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: It is an exception to this
retention period that Peer Review
Reports evaluating this agency's
program should be retained in
accordance with item number 77
(Audits).

series_metadata.series_title: Crime Stoppers Council – Proof of Certification
series_metadata.series_description: Includes letters, memoranda, or certificates proving certification of local Crime Stoppers programs by the Texas Crime Stoppers Council.

retention_rules.disposition: Permanent plus 0 years and 0 months

agendas and minutes of the Texas
Crime Stoppers Council.

series_metadata.series_title: Crime Stoppers Council - Certification Supporting Documentation
series_metadata.series_description: Includes applications, financial statements, annual Proof of Probation Fee and Repayment Reports (PFRR), training certificates, and contact information. Also includes copies of founding documents, tax- exempt forms, and other materials whose record copies are maintained by other state or federal agencies.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 1 year and 0 months

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 3 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Capital Access Program Project File - Enrolled
series_metadata.series_description: Lender enrollment folders include participation agreement documents (Exhibits 1-3), proof of deposit, copy of waiver (if applicable), checklist, required reports, and supporting documentation. May also include claim forms and recovery forms.

Program
administered in
accordance with
Government Code §§
481.401-.415 and 10
TAC §§ 187.1-.18.

series_metadata.series_title: Capital Access Program Project File - Not Enrolled
series_metadata.series_description: Participation agreement documents (Exhibits 1-3), copy of waiver (if applicable), checklist, required reports, and supporting documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 2 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_title: Events Trust Fund Programs - Administrative Files
series_metadata.series_description: Files used to administer state entities' participation in three event cost sharing trust accounts: Major Events Reimbursement Program, Motor Sports Racing Trust Fund, and Events Trust Fund. Documentation includes application documents and disbursement documents (requests, receipts, contracts) from applying entities (cities, counties, municipalities, organizations, other) related to an event eligible for the program. Documentation also includes funds management documentation including worksheets and event history that are associated with state and local participation.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Government
Code Chapter 478.
Major Events
Reimbursement
Program Texas
Government Code
Chapter 479. Motor
Sports Racing Trust
Fund Texas
Government Code
Chapter 480. Events
Trust Fund.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

series_metadata.series_description: Determination letters issued by the Regulatory Compliance division, agency submissions for review, and public comments received by the division regarding rules under review. An agency submission may include the Rule Submission Memorandum, proposed rule, public comments received, administrative records regarding the proposed rule, information requested by the division, and any other information that the state agency considers relevant to the division's review of the rule.
series_metadata.legal_citation:
Texas Occupations
Code, Chapter 57,
Subchapter C.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed

series_metadata.series_description: Loan agreements, promissory notes, quarterly reports, and related correspondence. Applies to Product Development/Small Business Incubator (PDSBI), Texas Leverage Fund (TLF), Texas Military Value Revolving Loan Fund (TMVRLF), Texas Small Business Industrial Development Corporation (TSBIDC), and any other loans awarded by the agency.

retention_rules.disposition: After Closed plus 7 years and 0 months

tracking_data.comments:
NOTE: Retention period applies to
agreements entered into on or after
September 1, 2015. An agreement
and related documentation entered
into prior to September 1, 2015
may be destroyed 4 years after the
expiration of termination of the
instrument according to its terms.
